heading: Why Extract Audio from Video
text: There are many legitimate reasons to extract audio from video files. Podcasters often repurpose video interviews into audio-only episodes for distribution on podcast platforms. Musicians extract audio from performance recordings to create demos or practice tracks.

heading: Copyright and Legal Considerations
text: Before extracting audio from any video, consider the copyright implications. Downloading or extracting audio from copyrighted content without permission is generally illegal, even if the content is freely available on platforms like YouTube. The fact that a video is publicly viewable does not grant you the right to download or redistribute its audio.

heading: Best Audio Formats for Different Uses
text: MP3 remains the most universally compatible audio format. At 192 kbps or higher, MP3 provides good quality for speech and music while keeping file sizes small. It works on virtually every device and platform, making it the safest choice for broad distribution.

heading: Extracting Audio with ToolPic
text: Open ToolPic's Extract Audio tool and upload the video file from which you want to extract the audio track. The tool supports MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, and other common video formats. Your video file stays on your device and is processed entirely in your browser.
